title: 2½″ Supply to Wye — Two Handlines
scene: Engine 181 has a 2½″ line stretched to a gated wye. Two 1¾″ handlines are operating from the wye.
studentQuestion: Calculate the pump pressure. Use the higher branch requirement, then add main-line friction loss and wye loss.
details: Main line: 200′ of 2½″ hose from engine to wye
Branch A: 200′ of 1¾″ hose, 165 gpm fog nozzle @ 50 psi
Branch B: 250′ of 1¾″ hose, 150 gpm fog nozzle @ 100 psi
instructorExplanation: Total flow through the 2½″ main is 315 gpm. Main FL = 2 × (315/100)² × 2 = 40 psi. Branch A needs 84 FL + 50 NP = 134 psi. Branch B needs 87 FL + 100 NP = 187 psi. Use the higher branch: 187 + 40 main FL + 10 wye loss = 237 psi.
explainMistake: Common errors: adding both branch pressures together, forgetting wye loss, or calculating the main line at only one branch flow instead of total flow.

variations.change: Make both branch nozzles 185 gpm @ 50 psi. Keep the same 200′ 2½″ main, 200′ Branch A, 250′ Branch B, and 10 psi wye loss.
variations.question: Same picture, new setup: Engine 181 is feeding the same wye. Both 1¾″ branch lines are now flowing 185 gpm at 50 psi. Branch A is 200′, Branch B is 250′, the 2½″ main is 200′, and wye loss is 10 psi. What pump pressure should you set?
details: Main line: 200′ of 2½″ hose from engine to wye
Branch A: 200′ of 1¾″ hose, 185 gpm fog nozzle @ 50 psi
Branch B: 250′ of 1¾″ hose, 185 gpm fog nozzle @ 50 psi
formulaBreakdown: Branch A FL = 15.5 × (185 ÷ 100)^2 × 2 = 106 psi; Branch A total = 156 psi
Branch B FL = 15.5 × (185 ÷ 100)^2 × 2.5 = 133 psi; Branch B total = 183 psi
Use the higher branch: 183 + 55 main FL + 10 wye loss = 248 psi
variations.instructorExplanation: For a wye, do not add both branch pressures. Use the higher branch requirement, then add the main-line friction loss and appliance loss.

variations.change: Add 20′ of elevation above the pump. Keep the original flows and hose lengths.
variations.question: Same picture, new setup: Use the original wye setup, but add 20′ of elevation above the pump. Use approximately +10 psi elevation pressure. What pump pressure should you set?
details: Main line: 200′ of 2½″ hose from engine to wye
Branch A: 200′ of 1¾″ hose, 165 gpm fog nozzle @ 50 psi
Branch B: 250′ of 1¾″ hose, 150 gpm fog nozzle @ 100 psi
formulaBreakdown: Elevation pressure = approximately +10 psi for 20′ above the pump
variations.instructorExplanation: This run reuses the same wye picture but adds elevation pressure to the final pump pressure.
